title: tabernaemontana cerea
intro: Tabernaemontana cerea, also known as the Wax Heart or White Correa, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various ailments such as skin irritations, wounds, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Tabernaemontana cerea can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for topical or internal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
